---
title: Paths
---

All paths in a *Calepin* document resolve from a single directory called the **project root**, with one exception: code chunks run from the input file's parent directory.

- Front matter fields (`bibliography`, `csl`, `css`), `{% include %}` directives, markdown image paths, and `_calepin/` overrides all resolve from the project root.
- Code chunks (R, Python, shell) run with their working directory set to the `.qmd` file's parent directory.

In **document mode** (`calepin render file.qmd`), the root is the directory containing the `.qmd` file -- so both rules point to the same place and every relative path means the same thing everywhere.

In **collection mode** (`calepin render _calepin.toml`), the root is the directory containing `_calepin.toml`. Code chunks still run from each `.qmd` file's own directory. This means YAML paths and code chunk paths can resolve from different locations when documents live in subdirectories.

*Calepin* prints the effective root at the start of each render (suppress with `-q`). When the two directories differ, both are shown. When you invoke a document-mode render from a different directory than the `.qmd` file's parent (e.g., `calepin render subdir/doc.qmd`), *Calepin* prints a note reminding you that the root is the input file's directory, not your shell's working directory.

# Input paths

Path-bearing front matter fields -- `bibliography`, `csl`, `css`, and `{% include %}` directives -- resolve relative to the root. Markdown image paths follow the same rule.

In document mode, this means paths are relative to the `.qmd` file's directory:

```yaml
bibliography: refs.bib          # same directory as the .qmd
csl: styles/apa.csl             # subdirectory
```

In collection mode, paths are relative to `_calepin.toml`, regardless of where the `.qmd` file sits in the project tree:

```yaml
bibliography: assets/library.bib   # from the project root, not the file's directory
```

This means every page in a collection uses the same path for shared resources, no matter how deeply nested.

# Code chunks

R, Python, and shell subprocesses run with their working directory set to the `.qmd` file's parent directory. This holds in both document and collection mode.

In document mode, this is the same as the project root, so there is no distinction.

In collection mode, if a `.qmd` file lives at `posts/2024/analysis.qmd`, its code chunks run from `posts/2024/` -- not the project root. So `read.csv("data.csv")` finds `data.csv` next to the source file, while `bibliography: refs.bib` in front matter resolves from the project root.

# Output paths

By default, output is written alongside the input file with the appropriate extension. The `-o` flag overrides this. Generated figures go in `_calepin/files/{stem}/` inside the output directory. The execution cache goes in `_calepin/cache/{stem}/` inside the root directory.

# Override resolution

Templates, element overrides, CSL files, and plugins follow a two-level fallback:

1. `_calepin/` in the root directory
2. Built-in defaults

Plugins resolve from `_calepin/plugins/{name}/plugin.toml`. Built-in plugins (tabset, layout, callout, theorem, figure-div, table-div) need no files on disk.

# Validation

*Calepin* validates `bibliography`, `csl`, and `calepin.plugins` paths before rendering. Missing files produce a hard error listing every unresolved path and showing the full resolved location that was tried. Template and element overrides are not validated -- they silently fall back to built-in defaults.

# Summary

| Path | Resolves from | On missing |
|------|--------------|------------|
| `bibliography`, `csl`, `css` | Project root | Hard error |
| `{% include %}` | Project root | Error in output |
| Images | Project root | -- |
| Code chunk working directory | `.qmd` file's parent | -- |
| Plugins | `_calepin/plugins/` | Hard error |
| Templates, elements | `_calepin/` | Falls back to built-in |
| Output file | Input directory or `-o` | Created |
| `_calepin/files/` | Output directory | Created |
| `_calepin/cache/` | Project root | Created |
